mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near the zero lower bound and stays flat or falls, discount rates remain compressed and equity valuations expand; this predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month because the pricing channel dominates without an offsetting tightening shock.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is flat or declining, or if the conditional average return is below the unconditional mean over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening labor income and higher recession probability, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A joint improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening payroll income and retail demand, which supports near-term corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS and RSAFS were both elevated in 2014-11
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-RSAFS signal is not positive in at least 55% of aligned observations or if its 60-day forward IC is below 0.02 for any 4-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO accelerates while CPIAUCSL is stable, real activity is rising without an inflation shock, which lowers recession risk and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month through better earnings visibility and unchanged policy pressure.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 106.6617 in 2014-11 while CPIAUCSL was 237.642 in 2014-10
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising INDPRO and flat CPIAUCSL do not outperform the unconditional 20-day SPX return by at least 0.5 percentage points, or if the sign is negative in a majority of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% with no offsetting inflation decline implies restrictive policy that raises discount rates and compresses valuation multiples, so S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 5-day forward SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if CPIAUCSL changes fail to materially alter the signal in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below its recent level signals weakening housing demand and a broader slowdown in consumer-led activity, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations soften.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdown triggers fail to predict below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the average triggered return is not negative in a rolling 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises while UNRATE falls, labor market breadth is improving and household income growth accelerates, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through stronger consumption expectations and lower recession odds.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 140045.0 and UNRATE was 5.8 in 2014-11
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down condition does not produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the effect disappears once either proxy is conditioned out.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising GDPC1 together with rising RSAFS identifies synchronized output and spending growth, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings revisions turn upward and cyclicals re-rate.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 and RSAFS were both above prior-cycle levels in the 2014 snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if synchronized rises in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to beat the unconditional 60-day SPX return, or if the joint signal is not positive in at least 60% of recovery episodes.
